hypothesis: If pre-trained machine learning interatomic potentials (eqV2 and eSEN) are used to generate approximate minimum energy pathways before DFT-based solid-state nudged elastic band refinement, then the total computational cost will be significantly reduced compared to pure DFT-based SSNEB calculations because the ML models can rapidly converge the initial pathway geometry, thereby substantially decreasing the number of expensive DFT SSNEB iterations required to reach the convergence threshold.
